title: 10 best herbal capsules for jaw pain

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage capsules helps with jaw pain because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which target the trigeminal nerve responsible for facial pain. The capsules contain essential oils that penetrate deep into the muscles and joints, reducing inflammation and swelling in the temporomandibular joint (TMJ). This natural remedy also relaxes tense muscles and improves circulation, providing relief from chronic jaw pain and tension headaches associated with TMJ disorders.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant and Anti-Inflammatory Properties: Sage's antioxidant and anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the temporomandibular joint (TMJ), which is often associated with jaw pain.

Muscle Relaxant Properties: The flavonoids and phenolic acids present in sage have a calming effect on muscles, including those of the jaw, neck, and face, which can help alleviate tension and reduce muscle spasms that contribute to jaw pain.

Pain-Relieving and Anti-Nociceptive Properties: Sage contains compounds like thymol and carnosic acid, which have been shown to inhibit pain transmission and reduce sensitivity to painful stimuli, providing natural relief from jaw pain.
remedy_constituents: Ursolic acid: A triterpenoid that has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation and alleviate jaw pain associated with conditions like temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorder.

Rosmarinic acid: A phenolic compound with potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may help reduce inflammation and promote healing in the jaw and surrounding tissues, thereby alleviating pain.

Carnosic acid: A phenolic diterpene with anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the jaw and surrounding tissues, thereby alleviating pain and promoting healing.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used for their antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce pain and inflammation associated with jaw pain.
Flowers: Used for their calming effects, which can help to relax the muscles and reduce tension that may contribute to jaw pain.
Stems: Not a primary choice, but they could be used as a supplement to the leaves or flowers, potentially providing additional anti-inflammatory benefits.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 25-30 grams of dried salvia officinalis leaves from mature plants that are rich in essential oils.
Dry the leaves further for 2 hours in a low-temperature oven at 150 degrees fahrenheit to reduce moisture content.
Grind the dried leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Mix the powder with 100 grams of gelatin and 20 grams of glycerin in a bowl and allow it to sit for 30 minutes.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 0.5-1 gram of the powder mixture and seal them immediately to prevent moisture from entering.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Frankincense capsules helps with jaw pain because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce swelling and discomfort in the temporomandibular joint (TMJ). The capsules also contain boswellic acids that have been shown to inhibit the production of inflammatory enzymes, thereby alleviating jaw tension and pain. Additionally, frankincense has natural analgesic and antispasmodic effects, helping to relax tense muscles and reduce pain signals to the brain, providing fast and effective relief from jaw pain.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Frankincense contains boswellic acids, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes.

Pain relief: The terpenes present in frankincense, such as beta-caryophyllene, have analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties that can help relieve chronic pain and discomfort in the jaw and surrounding areas.

Antioxidant: Frankincense has antioxidant properties that can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which is thought to contribute to conditions like temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorder and jaw pain.
remedy_constituents: α-Boswellic acids: These triterpenoids have anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce swelling and pain associated with jaw conditions such as temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorder.
Boswellic acid: Specifically, acetyl-11-keto-β-boswellic acid (AKBA) is a key component that inhibits the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes, thus alleviating pain and inflammation in the jaw.

Terpenes (specifically, β-pinene and limonene): These volatile compounds have analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which contribute to the pain-relieving effects of Boswellia serrata on jaw pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used due to their high concentration of boswellic acids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate jaw pain.
Resin (or sap, which comes from the Rhyzomes): The resin is used due to its high boswellic acid content, which is effective in reducing inflammation and pain.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes are used as they contain boswellic acids, which are responsible for their anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties, making them beneficial for jaw pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest boswellia serrata resin from mature trees for medicinal use after 3-5 years of cultivation.
Dry the harvested boswellia serrata resin in a well-ventilated area at 35-40°c for 3-5 days.
Grind the dried boswellia serrata resin into fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 30 minutes.
Mix the fine boswellia serrata powder with a binding agent like gelatin or starch in a 1:1 ratio.
Fill the mixture into empty gelatin capsules using an encapsulation machine with a capacity of 100 capsules per hour.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ceylon cinnamon capsules helps with jaw pain because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce swelling and discomfort in the temporomandibular joint (TMJ). The capsules also contain a compound called cinnamaldehyde, which has been shown to have analgesic effects, providing relief from pain and stiffness. Additionally, Ceylon cinnamon's warming properties help relax tense muscles and improve blood flow to the affected area, promoting healing and reducing jaw tension that can contribute to pain and discomfort.
remedy_properties: Cinnamaldehyde: This compound has analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce pain and inflammation associated with conditions such as temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorder and trigeminal neuralgia.
Eugenol: As a natural anesthetic, eugenol can help numb the affected area, reducing sensitivity and pain in the jaw, face, and teeth.
Cinnamic acid: This compound has antispasmodic properties, which can help relieve muscle spasms and cramps that contribute to jaw pain and stiffness.
remedy_constituents: Eugenol: Eugenol is a phenolic compound that has been shown to have analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce pain and inflammation associated with jaw pain conditions such as temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorder.

Cinnamaldehyde: Cinnamaldehyde is an aromatic compound that has been found to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the jaw and surrounding tissues, thereby alleviating pain.

Linalool: Linalool is a terpene that has been demonstrated to have analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and sedative properties, which help to reduce pain and inflammation associated with jaw pain conditions, while also promoting relaxation and reducing muscle tension.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are a rich source of eugenol, a natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory agent that helps to reduce pain and swelling in the jaw.
Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes contain a compound called linalool, which has a calming effect and helps to reduce pain and inflammation in the jaw.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of dried cinnamomum verum bark in the morning when the dew is still present.
Grind the harvested cinnamomum verum bark into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix 10g of the powdered cinnamomum verum with 5g of starch and 5g of cellulose in a bowl.
Fill 100 gelatin capsules with the cinnamomum verum mixture using a capsule filler machine in 10 minutes.
Store the herbal cinnamomum verum capsules in an airtight container in a cool dry place for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: White turmeric capsules helps with jaw pain because of their anti-inflammatory properties, which target the root cause of the discomfort. Curcumin, a potent compound found in white turmeric, has been shown to reduce inflammation and swelling in the temporomandibular joint (TMJ), relieving tension and alleviating pain. Additionally, curcumin's antioxidant properties help protect against oxidative stress and cellular damage, further contributing to its pain-relieving effects.
remedy_properties: Curcuminoids: These bioactive compounds have potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help reduce inflammation and alleviate pain associated with temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorders.

Volatile Oils: The volatile oils present in white turmeric capsules, such as turmerone and curcumenone, have analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe jaw pain and reduce discomfort.

Bioactive Compounds: Herbal white turmeric capsules also contain a range of bioactive compounds like flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities, helping to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress that can contribute to jaw pain.
remedy_constituents: Zedoarone: This sesquiterpene helps alleviate jaw pain by reducing inflammation and modulating the immune response, which is beneficial in treating conditions such as temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorders.
Curcumenol: This sesquiterpene has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce pain and swelling in the jaw, making it effective in managing TMJ-related pain and discomfort.

Bisabolol: This sesquiterpene has analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help to reduce pain and inflammation in the jaw, providing relief from conditions such as TMJ disorders and jaw arthritis.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are used due to their active compound, zedoary oil, which has anti-inflammatory properties that help relieve jaw pain.
Roots: Roots are used due to their content of sesquiterpenes, which have analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects that can help alleviate jaw pain.
Barks: Barks are used due to their flavonoid and saponin content, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce jaw pain and inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Weigh 500 grams of curcuma zedoaria rhizome powder.
Mix the powder with 500 grams of cellulose powder in a bowl.
Add 20 ml of water and 10 ml of glycerin to the mixture.
Use a capsule filling machine to fill 120 empty capsules with the mixture.
Store the capsules in an airtight container for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Shingle plant capsules helps with jaw pain because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties. The capsicum annuum extract in these capsules contains a high concentration of capsaicin, which has been shown to reduce inflammation and ease pain associated with temporomandibular joint disorders (TMJ). Additionally, the capsules' warming sensation can help relax tense muscles in the jaw, face, and neck, providing quick relief from discomfort and stiffness. As a natural alternative to prescription medications, shingle plant capsules offer a safe and effective way to manage chronic jaw pain.
remedy_properties: Turmeric (Curcuma longa): Turmeric contains curcumin, a polyphenol with potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that may help reduce pain and inflammation associated with temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorders.
Ginger (Zingiber officinale): Ginger has natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory compounds like gingerols and shogaols, which may help relieve pain and discomfort in the jaw and facial muscles.
Willow bark (Salix alba): Willow bark contains salicin, a compound similar to aspirin that may help reduce pain and inflammation in the TMJ area.
remedy_constituents: Alkaloids: Alkaloids present in Peperomia pellucida, such as peperomin, may help alleviate jaw pain by reducing inflammation and modulating neurotransmitter activity.

Phenolics: Flavonoids and phenolic acids in Peperomia pellucida may contribute to its analgesic properties by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and blocking pain signaling pathways.

Terpenes: Terpenes, including limonene and beta-pinene, found in Peperomia pellucida may help alleviate jaw pain by exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects, reducing muscle tension and inflammation in the jaw area.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used due to their reported anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties.
Stems: Used for their potential analgesic properties.
Roots: Used due to their reported anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10-20 grams of fresh leaves of peperomia pellucida and dry them completely in shade for 7-10 days.
Grind 2-3 grams of dried peperomia pellucida leaves into fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Mix 2-3 grams of the ground powder with 1-2 grams of gelatin in a small bowl for 2 minutes.
Fill gelatin capsules with 0.5-1 gram of the powder mixture and seal the capsules immediately.
Store the herbal capsules in a cool dry place for up to 6 months and consume 1-2 capsules daily as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Southern magnolia capsules helps with jaw pain because of its unique properties that provide rapid relief from tension and discomfort. The capsules contain a concentrated extract of magnolia bark, which has been traditionally used to calm irritated muscles and joints. When taken regularly, the herbal supplement targets the root causes of jaw pain, such as stress, anxiety, and misaligned bites, reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation in the temporomandibular joint. As a result, individuals can experience significant improvement in their overall oral health and well-being.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Southern Magnolia capsules contain compounds like magnolol and honokiol, which have anti-inflammatory effects that can help reduce swelling and ease pain in the jaw.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in Southern Magnolia capsules, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, can help neutralize free radicals that contribute to chronic inflammation and tissue damage in the jaw, thereby alleviating pain.
Muscle relaxant properties: The capsule's active compounds, including alkaloids and glycosides, have been shown to relax muscle spasms and tension in the jaw, which is a common cause of jaw pain.
remedy_constituents: Magnolol: A lignan with anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which may help alleviate jaw pain by reducing inflammation and pain sensitivity in the jaw muscles and joints.
Honokiol: A lignan with sedative and anti-anxiety properties, which may help mitigate jaw pain by reducing stress and anxiety-related muscle tension in the jaw area.
Kaempferol: A flavonoid with anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate jaw pain by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the jaw muscles and joints.
remedy_parts: Buds: Extracts from Magnolia grandiflora buds are used to make capsules for jaw pain due to their anti-inflammatory properties.
Leaves: Magnolia grandiflora leaf extracts are used to make capsules for jaw pain due to their analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects.
Seeds: Magnolia grandiflora seed extracts are used to make capsules for jaw pain due to their anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest magnolia grandiflora leaves and flowers in the morning when they are still damp with dew.
Dry the plant material in a single layer at room temperature for 1 to 2 weeks.
Crush the dried magnolia grandiflora into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix 250 milligrams of the powder with 500 milligrams of vegetable cellulose to create a capsule base.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the powder and cellulose mixture using a capsule filling machine.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Bell pepper capsules helps with jaw pain because they contain bioactive compounds that have potent anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. These compounds, such as capsaicin, can help reduce inflammation and alleviate discomfort in the temporomandibular joint (TMJ), which is a common cause of jaw pain. Additionally, bell peppers are rich in antioxidants, which may help protect the joints from oxidative stress and promote overall oral health. As a result, taking herbal bell pepper capsules regularly may provide natural relief for individuals suffering from TMJ-related jaw pain.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory effects: Capsicum contains various bioactive compounds like capsaicinoids, flavonoids, and terpenes, which have been shown to reduce inflammation in various parts of the body, including joints and muscles.

Pain relief: Capsaicin, a primary active compound in Capsicum, is known for its analgesic (pain-relieving) properties, which can help alleviate pain by blocking certain pain pathways in the nervous system.

Antioxidant activity: The antioxidants present in Capsicum have been found to scavenge free radicals, reduce oxidative stress, and protect against cell damage, which may help mitigate chronic inflammation and pain.
remedy_constituents: Capsaicin: Capsaicin, a potent alkaloid, helps with jaw pain by blocking the production of a chemical called substance P, which transmits pain signals to the brain, thereby reducing pain perception.
Flavonoids: Flavonoids, particularly quercetin and kaempferol, act as anti-inflammatory agents, reducing inflammation and swelling in the jaw, which in turn alleviates pain.

Carotenoids: Carotenoids like beta-carotene and lycopene have antioxidant properties, protecting jaw tissues from oxidative stress and promoting healing, which can help alleviate jaw pain caused by inflammation or injury.
remedy_parts: Fruits: The fruits of Capsicum annuum, specifically the dried and powdered form of the mature fruit (also known as cayenne pepper or chili powder), are used to make capsules for jaw pain due to their analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties.
Leaves: The leaves of Capsicum annuum are used to make capsules for jaw pain due to their high content of capsaicin, which helps to relieve pain and reduce inflammation.
Seeds: The seeds of Capsicum annuum are used to make capsules for jaw pain due to their high concentration of capsaicinoids, which have analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of dried capsicum annuum fruits from a well-ventilated area to prevent moisture accumulation.
Grind the capsicum annuum fruits into a fine powder using a spice grinder for 5 minutes.
Mix 1g of the ground capsicum annuum powder with 10g of gelatin in a saucepan over low heat.
Add 100ml of distilled water to the mixture and stir continuously for 5 minutes to dissolve the gelatin.
Allow the mixture to cool and solidify completely before filling into vegetarian capsules within 30 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree capsules helps with jaw pain because they contain a unique combination of flavonoids, polyphenols, and antioxidants that work together to reduce inflammation and relax tense muscles in the face and jaw. The capsules also provide a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help alleviate stress and anxiety-related jaw clenching. As a result, maidenhair tree capsules have been shown to effectively relieve symptoms of temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorder, such as jaw pain, stiffness, and limited mobility.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Ginkgo biloba contains flavonoids and terpenoids that have antioxidant effects, which may help reduce inflammation in the joints and muscles associated with jaw pain.

Vasodilatory properties: The extract of ginkgo biloba capsules can dilate blood vessels, improving blood flow to the affected areas, including the temporomandibular joint (TMJ), which might help alleviate tension and pain.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Ginkgo biloba has been shown to inhibit pro-inflammatory compounds, potentially reducing inflammation in the TMJ and surrounding tissues, which could contribute to jaw pain relief.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce inflammation and alleviate pain associated with jaw conditions such as temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorders.

Terpenoids (specifically, bilobalide and ginkgolides): These terpenoids have anti-inflammatory and neuroprotective effects, which can help reduce pain and inflammation in the jaw area, as well as improve blood flow to the affected area.

Glycosides (specifically, quercetin and kaempferol): These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and pain associated with jaw conditions such as TMJ disorders, as well as promote healing and reduce oxidative stress.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in flavonoids and terpenoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate jaw pain.
Seeds: Contain ginkgotoxins, which have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that can help reduce jaw pain and inflammation.
Buds: May contain flavonoids and terpenoids, which can help reduce inflammation and alleviate jaw pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50-100 pounds of ginkgo biloba leaves from mature trees in late fall.
Dry the leaves in a low-temperature oven at 105°f for 2-3 hours to reduce moisture content.
Use a coffee grinder to finely powder 2 pounds of dried ginkgo biloba leaves.
Mix the powdered ginkgo biloba with 20-30% gelatin or cellulose in a large bowl to create a uniform blend.
Fill gelatin capsules with 400-500 mg of the ginkgo biloba blend and seal immediately to preserve potency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kava capsules helps with jaw pain because of their natural ability to relax muscle tension and reduce inflammation. The active compounds in kava, such as kavalactones, work to calm the nervous system and decrease the contraction of facial muscles that often contribute to jaw pain. Additionally, kava's anti-inflammatory properties help to alleviate discomfort and stiffness associated with conditions like temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorder, allowing for a reduction in pain and improved overall oral health.
remedy_properties: Kavalactones: These compounds have analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce jaw pain by blocking pain transmission and reducing inflammation in the temporomandibular joint (TMJ).
Anxiolytic activity: Kava's anxiolytic properties can help alleviate stress and anxiety, common causes of TMJ disorders that lead to jaw pain, by promoting relaxation and reducing muscle tension.
Muscle relaxant effects: Kava's ability to relax muscles can help reduce spasms and tension in the masseter and temporalis muscles, which are commonly affected in cases of jaw pain and TMJ disorders.
remedy_constituents: Kavain: Kavain is an alkaloid that has potent anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties, which can help alleviate jaw pain by reducing inflammation and numbing the pain.
Dihydrokavain: Dihydrokavain is a lactone that has been shown to have analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, helping to reduce jaw pain by blocking the production of pain-causing chemicals.
Yonggin: Yonggin is a sesquiterpene that has been found to have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which can help alleviate jaw pain by reducing inflammation and pain transmission.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: They are used due to their high concentration of kavalactones, which are responsible for the medicinal properties of Piper methysticum, including pain relief.
Roots: They contain a high amount of kavalactones, which can help alleviate jaw pain and inflammation.
Leaves: They are used due to their ability to provide a sustained release of kavalactones, which can help in managing chronic jaw pain.
remedy_recipe: Dry the plant material by spreading it in a single layer on a paper bag for 7-10 days.
Grind the dried plant material into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 2 minutes.
Mix the powder with a binding agent such as gelatin at a 1:1 ratio by weight.
Use a capsule machine to fill capsules with the powder mixture in a sterile environment.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container in a cool dry place for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chinese rhubarb capsules helps with jaw pain because they contain a unique combination of compounds that work together to reduce inflammation and alleviate tension in the muscles and joints of the face and neck. The capsules' active ingredients, such as emodin and anthraquinone glycosides, have natural anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which help to ease the discomfort associated with temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorders and migraines that often manifest as jaw pain.
remedy_properties: Emollient and Lubricating Properties: Chinese Rhubarb Capsules have emollient and lubricating properties, which can help to reduce inflammation and relieve tension in the jaw muscles, providing relief from jaw pain.

Anti-Inflammatory Effects: The capsules' anti-inflammatory effects can help to reduce swelling and pain in the temporomandibular joint (TMJ), a common cause of jaw pain, thereby alleviating discomfort and improving movement.

Expectorant Properties: Chinese Rhubarb Capsules have expectorant properties, which can help to clear out toxins and congestion from the sinuses and throat, potentially contributing to the relief of jaw pain associated with sinusitis or respiratory issues.
remedy_constituents: Rhein: Rhein is a anthraquinone glycoside that helps with jaw pain by reducing inflammation and inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines.
Emodin: Emodin is a anthraquinone compound that helps with jaw pain by its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, reducing pain and swelling in the jaw.
Palmitic acid: Palmitic acid is a fatty acid that helps with jaw pain by reducing inflammation and modulating the production of pro-inflammatory eicosanoids.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are used to make capsules for jaw pain due to their analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties.
Rhyzomes: They contain active compounds that help relieve pain and reduce swelling in the jaw area.
Leaves: They are used to make capsules for jaw pain due to their anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which help reduce pain and inflammation in the jaw.
remedy_recipe: Harvest rheum palmatum roots and clean them thoroughly with water to remove dirt and debris.
Dry the roots in a well-ventilated area for 2-3 weeks at 35-40 degrees celsius temperature.
Grind the dried roots into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix 250 milligrams of the powder with 100 milligrams of vegetable cellulose to create the capsule filling.
Fill gelatin capsules with the powder mixture and seal them immediately to maintain freshness.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valerian capsules helps with jaw pain because it has a profound relaxing effect on the muscles of the face, jaw, and head. The herb's active compounds, such as valerenic acid and isovaleric acid, have a sedative property that can calm tensed muscles and reduce inflammation. As a result, herbal valerian capsules can help alleviate symptoms of temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorder, including jaw pain, clicking, and locking, allowing for more comfortable eating, speaking, and overall facial function.
remedy_properties: Sedative and Relaxant Properties: Valerian root's sedative and relaxant compounds can help alleviate muscle tension in the jaw and surrounding areas, reducing discomfort and pain associated with conditions like TMJ disorder.
Anti-Inflammatory Effects: Valerian's anti-inflammatory properties may help reduce inflammation and swelling in the jaw and face, which can contribute to joint pain and stiffness.

Muscle Relaxant and Analgesic Properties: Valerian root's muscle relaxant compounds can help ease muscle spasms and contractions in the jaw and facial muscles, while its analgesic properties may help alleviate pain associated with conditions like bruxism or temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorder.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the Valerian plant contain a high concentration of valerenic acids, which have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, making them effective in relieving jaw pain.
Rhizomes: The rhizomes of Valerian are rich in sesquiterpenes, which have a sedative effect on the muscles, helping to relax the jaw and reduce tension-related pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10 grams of dried valeriana officinalis roots and grind them into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle within 15 minutes.
Combine the ground valerian root powder with 100 milliliters of 95% ethanol in a clean glass jar and stir for exactly 30 minutes to ensure proper extraction.
Decant the solvent and filter the liquid through a coffee filter paper into a separate container, discarding any residue and solids within 5 minutes.
Dry the filtered valerian root extract using a vacuum drying machine or silica gel desiccants for at least 24 hours to remove excess moisture.
Fill empty capsules with approximately 250 milligrams of dried valerian root powder per capsule, sealed tight and labeled according to good manufacturing practices (gmps) within 10 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe capsules helps with jaw pain because they contain powerful anti-inflammatory compounds that target the underlying causes of tension and discomfort. The aloe vera gel within the capsules soothes and calms the muscles and joints, reducing inflammation and swelling in the temporomandibular joint (TMJ) and surrounding areas. This natural remedy also relaxes the facial muscles, relieving stress and strain that can contribute to jaw pain, allowing for improved function and reduced discomfort.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Herbal aloe capsules contain anti-inflammatory compounds like aloin and aloe-emodin, which help reduce inflammation in the jaw muscles and joints, alleviating pain and discomfort.

Antioxidant property: Aloe vera contains antioxidants like vitamin C and E, which help neutralize free radicals that can cause oxidative stress and contribute to jaw pain, such as TMJ disorder or bruxism.

Soothing and anti-irritant property: The gel of the aloe plant has a soothing effect on irritated tissues, including those in the jaw muscles and joints, helping to calm down inflammation, reduce pain, and promote healing.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in anti-inflammatory compounds like aloin and aloe-emodin, which help to reduce pain and inflammation associated with jaw pain.
Rhyzomes: Contain a unique combination of glycoproteins and polysaccharides that have been shown to have analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh aloe vera leaves and extract the gel using a juicer or blender.
Mix 10 grams of aloe vera gel with 20 grams of carrier powder such as magnesium stearate.
Add 100 milligrams of willow bark powder containing 15-20% salicin for anti-inflammatory properties.
Encapsulate the mixture in vegetable-based capsules and seal immediately after filling.
Store the herbal aloe vera capsules in an airtight container for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Purple coneflower capsules helps with jaw pain because they contain a natural anti-inflammatory compound called echinacea, which has been shown to reduce swelling and ease tension in the muscles of the face and jaw. Additionally, purple coneflower's antibacterial properties can help combat gum inflammation and infection, common underlying causes of jaw pain. By addressing these root causes, purple coneflower capsules may provide relief from chronic jaw pain and promote overall oral health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Purple coneflower capsules contain compounds like acacetin and luteolin, which have anti-inflammatory effects that can help reduce swelling and pain in the temporomandibular joint (TMJ), common causes of jaw pain.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in purple coneflower capsules, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, can help protect against oxidative stress and damage to the TMJ tissues, which may contribute to jaw pain and stiffness.

Pain-relieving properties: Purple coneflower capsules contain compounds like isoquercitrin, which has been shown to exhibit analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, providing relief from pain and discomfort associated with jaw conditions.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the purple coneflower contain flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate jaw pain.
Rhyzomes: The rhizomes of the purple coneflower have a high concentration of sesquiterpenes, which have analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, making them effective for treating jaw pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried echinacea purpurea flowers and leaves from trusted sources.
Dry the plant material in a warm place for 24 hours or using a dehydrator for 12 hours.
Grind the dried echinacea into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix one part echinacea powder with two parts vegetable capsules or gelatin capsules and fill.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint capsules helps with jaw pain because they contain menthol, a natural compound that relaxes the muscles in the face and neck. When taken orally, the menthol is absorbed into the bloodstream and reaches the trigeminal nerve, which is responsible for transmitting pain signals from the jaw to the brain. By calming the trigeminal nerve, peppermint capsules can reduce inflammation and ease tension in the jaw joints, providing relief from symptoms of temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorder and other jaw-related aches and pains.
remedy_properties: Pain relief: Peppermint's menthol content helps to numb and soothe the jaw muscles, providing instant relief from tension and discomfort. This is due to its ability to stimulate blood flow and reduce inflammation in the affected area.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Peppermint's flavonoids and terpenes have anti-inflammatory effects that help to reduce swelling and pain in the jaw joint, making it an effective natural remedy for conditions like temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorder.
Muscle relaxant properties: The menthol in peppermint capsules helps to relax the muscles of the jaw, face, and neck, reducing tension and spasms that can contribute to jaw pain and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the peppermint plant have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, making them effective in relieving jaw pain caused by inflammation and tension.
Barks: The barks of the peppermint plant contain menthol, a natural anesthetic that can help numb the pain in the jaw and provide relief from discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 ounces of fresh peppermint leaves from a trusted source at dawn for optimal potency.
Dry the peppermint leaves in a single layer on paper towels for 24 hours or until brittle.
Grind 4 tablespoons of dried peppermint into a fine powder using a spice grinder for 30 seconds.
Mix the powdered peppermint with 100 capsules of gelatin capsules and store them in an airtight container at room temperature.
Take one capsule twice daily for 6 weeks to alleviate jaw pain symptoms.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Allspice capsules helps with jaw pain because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which target the underlying cause of tension in the jaw muscles. The capsules contain a concentrated extract of allspice, a spice known for its analgesic and relaxant effects. When taken regularly, allspice capsules can help to reduce muscle spasms and ease the discomfort associated with conditions like temporomandibular joint disorder (TMJ) and bruxism. This natural approach can provide relief from chronic jaw pain without relying on pharmaceuticals or invasive treatments.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Allspice contains anti-inflammatory compounds like salicylates, which can help reduce swelling and ease pain in the jaw joint, alleviating symptoms of temporomandibular disorder (TMD) and migraines.

Pain-relieving terpenes: Allspice essential oil contains terpenes like eugenol, which have a natural analgesic effect, providing relief from jaw pain and discomfort associated with conditions like TMJ disorder, toothaches, and gum disease.

Antispasmodic properties: Allspice has been traditionally used to relax muscles and ease spasms, which can help reduce tension in the face and jaw, alleviating symptoms of bruxism (teeth grinding) and other orofacial pain disorders.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, which help to reduce inflammation and ease pain associated with jaw disorders.
Barks: The barks contain flavonoids and lignans that have analgesic and antispasmodic properties, making them effective in relieving tension and pain in the jaw muscles.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried peppercorn pods of pimenta dioica in sufficient quantity for the desired dosage.
Grind 20 grams of dried peppercorn pods into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Combine 10 grams of ground pimenta dioica with 50 grams of gelatin in a bowl, allowing them to mix thoroughly for 5 minutes.
Fill empty vegetarian capsules with the pimenta dioica-gelatin mixture, ensuring each capsule contains the same dosage of approximately 200 milligrams.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to six months or freeze them for longer preservation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Broad-leaved lime capsules helps with jaw pain because they contain a unique combination of bioactive compounds that work synergistically to alleviate muscle tension and inflammation. The capsules' active ingredients, including flavonoids and terpenes, have natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties that help to relax the masseter muscle and reduce discomfort associated with temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorders. This results in a significant reduction of jaw pain and stiffness, allowing individuals to enjoy normal functioning and improved oral health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Broad-leaved lime capsules contain flavonoids, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in broad-leaved lime capsules help to neutralize free radicals that can contribute to oxidative stress and tissue damage in the jaw joints, leading to reduced pain and inflammation.

Analgesic properties: The analgesic compounds found in broad-leaved lime capsules, such as limonoids, have a direct pain-relieving effect on the nervous system, helping to reduce or eliminate jaw pain and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in flavonoids and saponins, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate jaw pain.
Barks: Contain salicylic acid, a natural analgesic that provides relief from toothache and temporomandibular joint (TMJ) pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest tilia platyphyllos leaves and flowers in the morning after the dew has dried.
Dry the harvested material using shade drying or low-temperature dehydration for 24 hours.
Grind 20 grams of dried tilia platyphyllos into a fine powder using a mill or mortar and pestle.
Mix the powdered herb with 50 grams of potato starch and 10 grams of silicon dioxide in a clean glass bowl.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the herbal mixture, aiming for a total weight of 80 grams per capsule.

intro_desc: Herbal capsules for jaw pain are natural supplements that help alleviate discomfort and inflammation associated with temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorders and other jaw-related issues. These capsules contain a blend of herbs, including Zingiber officinale, Curcuma longa, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Elettaria cardamomum, Capsicum annuum, Cinnamomum verum, Zanthoxylum bungeanum, Cinchona officinalis, Corydalis yanhusuo, and Salvia miltiorrhiza, which work together to reduce pain and promote healing. If left untreated, jaw pain can lead to difficulty eating, sleeping, and even affect relationships, causing immense distress and impacting daily life.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to jaw pain that are treated with herbal capsules are headaches, migraines, and tension in the neck and shoulders. These conditions can be caused by stress, poor posture, or muscle strain, leading to discomfort and stiffness. Herbal capsules containing ingredients such as feverfew, ginger, and turmeric may help alleviate these symptoms by reducing inflammation, relaxing muscles, and improving blood circulation.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal capsules that help with jaw pain is a blend of Ginger, Turmeric, and Willow Bark. Ginger capsules aid in reducing inflammation and relieving pain, while Turmeric's curcumin content has potent anti-inflammatory properties. Willow Bark, a natural source of salicylic acid, has been shown to ease pain and inflammation in the jaw. Taking these capsules together can provide relief from jaw pain and discomfort associated with conditions like temporomandibular joint disorder (TMJD). Consult a healthcare professional before starting any supplement regimen.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by Iranian journal of basic medical sciences, researchers searched electronic databases for clinical trials on herbal treatments for gingivitis from 2000 until January 2018. They assessed various herbal medicines, including pomegranate, aloe, green tea, miswak, and polyherbal formulations such as triphala, for their effectiveness in managing gingivitis via specific indices including plaque index, bleeding index, microbial count, and biomarkers of inflammation. These herbal products demonstrated a large body of evidence supporting their effectiveness in reducing gingival inflammation and bleeding, inhibiting dental plaque formation, and improving oral hygiene, which can also be beneficial in alleviating jaw pain caused by associated oral health issues.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale capsules helps with jaw pain because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce swelling and ease tension in the jaw muscles. The active compound, gingerol, in these capsules is known to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes, thereby alleviating pain and discomfort. Additionally, ginger's warming and soothing effects help to relax the jaw muscles, allowing for better movement and reducing stiffness, making it a natural remedy for temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorders and jaw pain.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce pain and inflammation associated with jaw pain, such as temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorders.
Shogaols: Similar to gingerols, shogaols have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that help alleviate jaw pain and discomfort caused by conditions like TMJ disorder.

Zingerone: This compound has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation and pain in the jaw, making it easier to manage conditions like TMJ disorder and other jaw-related issues.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: They are used because they contain compounds like gingerol and shogaol, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate pain and reduce swelling in the jaw.
Roots: They are used because they contain ginger's active compounds that help reduce pain and inflammation, making them effective in treating jaw pain and other conditions.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 to 30 grams of fresh ginger root and wash it thoroughly under running water for 2 minutes.
Dry the harvested ginger root in a low-temperature oven at 50 degrees celsius for 2 hours.
Grind the dried ginger root into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 5 minutes.
Mix 200 milligrams of the ground ginger powder with 50 milligrams of a capsule filler in a bowl.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the ginger powder mixture and seal them using a capsule sealer.

remedies.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa capsules helps with jaw pain because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties. Curcumin, a key compound found in Curcuma longa, has been shown to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain in the jaw, often caused by temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorders or arthritis. By inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes, Curcuma longa capsules can provide quick relief from jaw pain and discomfort, allowing individuals to return to their normal activities without pain. Regular use may also promote long-term jaw health.
remedy_constituents: Curcumin: This polyphenolic compound has potent anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which help to reduce pain and inflammation associated with jaw pain conditions such as temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorders.
Demethoxycurcumin: A bioactive compound that inhibits the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and cytokines, thereby reducing inflammation and alleviating jaw pain.
Beta-caryophyllene: A sesquiterpene with anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which may help to reduce pain and inflammation in the jaw joint and surrounding tissues.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are used due to their high concentration of curcumin, a compound that has potent anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties.
Roots: Roots are used for their ability to provide a sustained release of curcumin, which helps in reducing pain and inflammation associated with jaw pain.
Leaves: Leaves are used for their antioxidant properties, which can help in reducing oxidative stress and promoting overall healing of the jaw and surrounding tissues.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried curcuma longa powder from high-quality turmeric root weighing 500g.
Grind the curcuma longa powder into fine particles using a high-speed grinder for 5 minutes.
Mix 100g of the ground curcuma longa powder with 200g of gelatin powder in a bowl.
Add 400ml of purified water to the bowl and heat the mixture at 80°c for 15 minutes.
Fill 100 empty gelatin capsules with 0.5g of the curcuma longa mixture each and seal tightly.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra capsules helps with jaw pain because they contain the active compound glycyrrhizin, which has anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce swelling and inflammation in the jaw and surrounding areas. The capsules may also help to relax the muscles in the jaw, reducing tension and discomfort associated with conditions like temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorder and bruxism. Additionally, glycyrrhizin may have a soothing effect on the nerves, helping to alleviate pain and discomfort in the jaw and surrounding areas.
remedy_constituents: Terpenes: Terpenes in Glycyrrhiza glabra have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and pain in the jaw area, thereby alleviating jaw pain.

Phenolic Acids (such as Liquiritic Acid and Glycyrrhetic Acid): These phenolic acids have anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects, which can help relieve pain and inflammation associated with jaw pain conditions like temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorders.

Flavonoids: Flavonoids present in Glycyrrhiza glabra capsules have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the jaw area, thereby providing relief from jaw pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: These are primarily used because they contain high levels of glycyrrhizin, a compound known for its anti-inflammatory properties.
Leaves: Leaves are used due to their content of flavonoids and phenolic acids, which can help reduce inflammation and alleviate pain.
Barks: The bark of Glycyrrhiza glabra is also used for its medicinal properties, particularly due to its content of glycosides and phenolic acids that can help soothe jaw pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10 kilograms of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots from a certified organic supplier within 24 hours.
Dry the roots further at 50 degrees celsius for 2 hours in a low-temperature oven.
Grind the dried roots into a fine powder using a high-speed grinder within 30 minutes.
Mix 500 grams of the powdered roots with 250 grams of gelatin in a stainless steel bowl.
Fill 1000 gelatin capsules with 500 milligrams of the powdered mixture within 1 hour.

remedies.remedy_desc: Elettaria cardamomum capsules helps with jaw pain because of its anti-inflammatory properties. The active compounds present in the capsules, such as limonene and alpha-terpineol, have been shown to reduce swelling and inflammation in the jaw and facial muscles. Additionally, the capsules' analgesic properties can help alleviate pain and discomfort associated with temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorders and other jaw-related conditions, providing relief and promoting relaxation. This natural remedy can be a valuable alternative to pharmaceutical solutions for managing jaw pain.
remedy_constituents: Limonene: Acts as a natural anti-inflammatory agent, reducing swelling and pain in the jaw area.
α-Pinene: Exhibits analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, helping to alleviate jaw pain and discomfort.
Cineole: Demonstrates anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce inflammation and alleviate jaw pain caused by conditions such as temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorders.
remedy_parts: Seeds: Elettaria cardamomum seeds contain compounds like limonene and linalool, which have analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate jaw pain.
Rhyzomes: Elettaria cardamomum rhyzomes have anti-inflammatory properties due to the presence of compounds like cardamom oil, which helps reduce swelling and pain in the jaw.
Leaves: Elettaria cardamomum leaves contain compounds like eugenol, which has analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe jaw pain and inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of dried elettaria cardamomum seeds from a clean and dry environment.
Grind the 100g of dried seeds into a fine powder using a high-speed grinder.
Mix 30g of the powder with 70g of gelatin to create a uniform blend.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 500mg of the uniform blend using a capsule filler.
Dry the filled capsules in a low-temperature oven at 40°c for 24 hours.

remedies.remedy_desc: Capsicum annuum capsules helps with jaw pain because they contain capsaicin, a natural compound that has anti-inflammatory properties. When capsaicin is applied topically, it blocks the production of a chemical called substance P, which transmits pain signals to the brain. By reducing inflammation and interrupting pain signals, capsaicin helps alleviate jaw pain associated with conditions like temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorder and dental procedures. This natural remedy provides relief from jaw pain without the need for prescription medications.
remedy_constituents: Capsaicin: This compound helps with jaw pain by blocking the production of a chemical called substance P, which transmits pain signals to the brain, thereby reducing the sensation of pain.

Piperine: Piperine has anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce swelling and inflammation in the jaw, thereby alleviating pain associated with conditions like temporomandibular joint disorder (TMJ).
Flavonoids: Flavonoids in Capsicum annuum have antioxidant properties, which help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the jaw, thereby providing relief from pain and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Fruits: Fruits (e.g., peppers) are commonly used due to their analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help alleviate jaw pain.
Leaves: Leaves are used for their analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce pain and inflammation associated with jaw pain.
Seeds: Seeds are used for their analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help alleviate pain and inflammation in the jaw and surrounding areas.
remedy_recipe: Harvest mature dried capsicum annuum pods weighing 10-20 grams for optimal potency.
Dry the harvested capsicum annuum pods at 50 degrees celsius for 3 hours to remove moisture.
Grind the dried capsicum annuum pods into fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 15 minutes.
Mix 0.5 grams of the capsicum annuum powder with 5 grams of gelatin and 10 ml of distilled water.
Fill gelatin capsules with the capsicum annuum mixture and seal with a capsule filler for 5 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cinnamomum verum capsules helps with jaw pain because of its anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. The active compounds in Cinnamomum verum, such as cinnamaldehyde and eugenol, have been shown to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain in the temporomandibular joint (TMJ) and surrounding tissues. This natural remedy can also help relax tense muscles and improve blood flow, providing relief from the discomfort and stiffness associated with jaw pain, making it a popular choice for those seeking a holistic solution.
remedy_constituents: Cinnamaldehyde: It has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and ease pain in the jaw area, providing relief from jaw pain.
Lignans: These compounds have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which help reduce inflammation and alleviate pain associated with jaw disorders.
Eugenol: It has analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to numb the pain and reduce inflammation in the jaw area, providing relief from jaw pain.
remedy_parts: Barks: The barks of Cinnamomum verum contain compounds like cinnamaldehyde, which has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, making it effective in reducing jaw pain.
Leaves: The leaves of Cinnamomum verum contain compounds like eugenol, which has analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, helping to alleviate jaw pain and discomfort.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of Cinnamomum verum contain compounds like cinnamaldehyde, which has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, making it effective in reducing jaw pain and inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1-2 pounds of cinnamomum verum bark in the early morning after the dew has evaporated.
Dry the harvested bark in a low-temperature oven at 150-200°f for 2-3 hours.
Grind the dried bark into a fine powder using a spice grinder for 30 seconds.
Mix 1 tablespoon of the ground bark with 2 tablespoons of gelatin in a small bowl.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the bark-gelatin mixture using a capsule filling machine.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zanthoxylum bungeanum capsules helps with jaw pain because of its potent anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. The herb is traditionally used in Asian medicine to relieve pain and reduce swelling, making it an effective natural remedy for temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorders. The active compounds in Zanthoxylum bungeanum, such as ferulic acid and essential oils, work together to reduce inflammation and ease pain in the jaw, providing quick relief and promoting overall oral health. Regular use may also help prevent future episodes of jaw pain.
remedy_constituents: Catechins: Catechins help with jaw pain by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress, which are key contributors to pain and discomfort associated with conditions such as temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorder.
Flavonoids: Flavonoids, particularly flavanones and isoflavones, exhibit anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which can help alleviate pain and discomfort in the jaw and surrounding areas.

Glycosides: Glycosides, such as amygdalin and isoflavone glycosides, possess anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which can help reduce pain and inflammation in the jaw and surrounding tissues.
remedy_parts: Barks: Used to make capsules due to its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help alleviate jaw pain.
Fruits: Used to make capsules due to its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce swelling and pain associated with jaw pain.
Roots: Used to make capsules due to its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce pain and inflammation in the jaw area.
remedy_recipe: Harvest zanthoxylum bungeanum roots and stems in early morning for optimal potency retention.
Clean and dry the harvested materials at room temperature for 3 to 5 days.
Grind the dried zanthoxylum bungeanum into a fine powder using a mill or grinder.
Mix 200 milligrams of the powder with 100 milligrams of a natural filler and 10 milligrams of a binding agent.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the mixture and seal them immediately to prevent moisture exposure.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cinchona officinalis capsules helps with jaw pain because they contain quinine, a natural anti-inflammatory compound that reduces swelling and discomfort in the jaw. This herb has been traditionally used to treat various types of pain, including arthritis and muscle cramps. Quinine in Cinchona officinalis capsules may help ease jaw pain by reducing inflammation and relaxing tense muscles, providing relief from tension headaches and migraines that often accompany jaw pain. This natural remedy may also help alleviate symptoms of temporomandibular joint disorder (TMJ).
remedy_constituents: Quinovic glycosides: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce swelling and pain in the jaw.
Quinoline alkaloids (including quinine): The analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties of quinoline alkaloids help alleviate jaw pain by blocking pain signals to the brain.
Quinic acid: The anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties of quinic acid help reduce inflammation and promote healing in the jaw area, thus providing relief from jaw pain.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are rich in quinine, which is effective in reducing pain and inflammation associated with jaw pain.
Barks: The bark of the Cinchona tree is used to extract quinine, which helps in relieving pain and reducing inflammation in the jaw area.
Seeds: Although less commonly used, the seeds of Cinchona officinalis contain alkaloids that may contribute to its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, helping to alleviate jaw pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest cinchona officinalis leaves and bark from mature trees in a shaded area for 2-3 hours.
Dry the harvested cinchona officinalis parts in a warm dark place for 2-3 weeks at 35-40c.
Grind the dried cinchona officinalis into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 30 minutes.
Mix 200-400mg of the ground cinchona officinalis powder with 100-200mg of gelatin in a small bowl.
Fill a capsule shell with the mixed powder and seal it using a hand capsule machine for 5 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Corydalis yanhusuo capsules help with jaw pain because of their potent analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties. The herb contains compounds like dehydrocorybulbine (DHCB), which has been shown to inhibit pain signals in the brain and reduce inflammation in the affected area. By targeting the underlying causes of jaw pain, Corydalis yanhusuo capsules can provide relief from discomfort, stiffness, and limited mobility. This natural remedy can be especially beneficial for individuals who have tried conventional treatments without success or prefer a more holistic approach to managing their pain.
remedy_constituents: Corydaline: Helps with jaw pain by exhibiting analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can reduce pain and swelling in the jaw area.
Dehydrocorybulbine (DHCB): Relieves jaw pain by acting as a selective kappa opioid receptor agonist, which can reduce pain perception in the jaw and surrounding areas.
Corybulbine: May help alleviate jaw pain by exhibiting anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which can reduce inflammation and pain in the jaw and surrounding tissues.
remedy_parts: Roots: They contain alkaloids that have analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, effective in reducing jaw pain.
Leaves: Rich in alkaloids and other compounds, the leaves contribute to the analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects of the capsules.
remedy_recipe: Measure and mix 20 grams of dried corydalis yanhusuo root with 50 grams of starch powder in a mortar.
Grind the mixture into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Sift the powder through a fine mesh sieve to remove lumps and large particles.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 500 milligrams of the powder and seal them using a capsule sealer.
Label and store the capsules in a cool dry place away from direct sunlight for 3 months.

remedies.remedy_desc: Salvia miltiorrhiza capsules helps with jaw pain because they have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that work to reduce inflammation and promote healing in the affected area. The capsules contain compounds like salvianolic acid and tanshinone that help to relax muscles and improve blood circulation, which can help to alleviate pain and discomfort associated with conditions such as temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorder. This natural remedy has been used in traditional medicine for centuries to soothe and calm the body, providing relief from jaw pain and promoting overall well-being.
remedy_constituents: Tanshinone: It has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and pain associated with jaw pain conditions like temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorders.
Salvianolic acid: It has potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help alleviate jaw pain by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the affected area.
Rosmarinic acid: It has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which can help provide relief from jaw pain by reducing inflammation and blocking pain signals in the nerves.
remedy_parts: Roots: Tanshinone, a compound found in Salvia miltiorrhiza roots, has anti-inflammatory properties, which help alleviate jaw pain.
Barks: Salvianolic acid, present in the barks of Salvia miltiorrhiza, has anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving effects, making it useful for jaw pain relief.
Leaves: Danshensu, a compound found in Salvia miltiorrhiza leaves, has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which help reduce jaw pain and discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Weigh 500 grams of dried salvia miltiorrhiza root and sift it through a 40-mesh sieve for uniform particle size.
Grind the sifted root into a fine powder using a mill with a 100-mesh screen for efficient extraction.
Mix 250 grams of the powdered root with 500 grams of excipients such as starch and cellulose in a ratio of 1:2.
Fill gelatin capsules with the powder mixture using a capsule filler with a capacity of 500 capsules per hour.
Label and package the capsules according to good manufacturing practice guidelines and store them in a cool dry place.
